title: KIA Landmine Ambush Kills ~40 Pro-Junta Militia in Hpakant — Major Single-Action Losses for SAC
detail: At approximately 8:00 AM on April 19, 2026, Kachin Independence Army (KIA) forces conducted a landmine ambush on a pro-junta militia column in Hpakant Township, Kachin State, killing approximately 40 militia members. The ambush struck a militia patrol in the same area that has seen sustained KIA-SAC fighting since April 11, following KIA's capture of six SAC frontline positions near Maw Wan Gyi on April 2. Pro-junta militia units deployed in Hpakant — Border Guard Force-affiliated fighters and locally recruited auxiliaries — have increasingly been thrown into combat as the regular SAC suffers attrition from sustained KIA operations. The loss of ~40 militia in a single engagement is among the highest single-action casualty counts in Hpakant in 2026 and represents a significant degradation of the junta's militia network in Kachin. Hpakant generates an estimated $2–3 billion annually in jade revenue for the junta, making sustained KIA operations there both militarily and economically devastating to SAC capacity.

media.caption: KIA landmine ambush kills ~40 pro-junta militia in Hpakant Township, Kachin State, at 8:00 AM on April 19

title: SAC Drops 500-Pound Bombs on Mrauk-U and Ponnagyun — 13 Civilians Injured Including 3 Monks
detail: On April 19, 2026, Myanmar Air Force aircraft dropped 500-pound bombs on Mrauk-U and Ponnagyun townships in Rakhine State, injuring at least 13 civilians including 3 monks, 8 men, and 2 women. Mrauk-U — the ancient Rakhine capital and UNESCO World Heritage candidate — has been under Arakan Army (AA) civil administration since February 2024. Ponnagyun Township lies between Mrauk-U and the besieged state capital Sittwe. The strikes represent continued SAC aerial harassment of AA-controlled Rakhine State, where the junta has lost governance and territorial control but retains air power. The targeting of monks echoes the April 13 monastery bombing in Sagaing Region, and the repeated strikes on AA-held Rakhine indicate the SAC is using airpower as its primary instrument of pressure in territories it can no longer hold on the ground. The strikes injure civilians in a region where the AA maintains civil governance, courts, taxation, and service provision.

media.caption: SAC drops 500-pound bombs on Mrauk-U and Ponnagyun, Rakhine State, injuring 13 including 3 monks on April 19

title: Inter-Parliamentary Union Confirms CRPH as Myanmar's Principal Parliamentary Representative
detail: On April 19, 2026, the Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU) — the global organization of national parliaments with 180 member states — confirmed its recognition of Myanmar's Committee Representing Pyidaungsu Hluttaw (CRPH) as the country's principal parliamentary representative body. The IPU decision explicitly rejects the legitimacy of the junta's December 2025 elections and the parliament formed under SAC supervision. The CRPH was established on February 5, 2021 by elected NLD lawmakers who evaded arrest following the coup; it later provided the legal and constitutional basis for the National Unity Government (NUG). IPU recognition grants CRPH access to inter-parliamentary forums, multilateral observer status, and significant symbolic legitimacy in international diplomatic settings. The IPU's reaffirmation comes as the resistance deepens its governance structures through the SCEF (Steering Council for Federal Democratic Union, formed March 30, 2026) and as TNLA's April 15 endorsement of Min Aung Hlaing's government fractures the Three Brotherhood Alliance — making the IPU's reinforcement of resistance legitimacy particularly timely.

media.caption: IPU confirms recognition of CRPH as Myanmar's principal parliamentary representative body on April 19, 2026
